picks.reason: Gray is the superior arm (3.24 xFIP, 19.6 K-BB%, 243 IP sample) facing Yesavage who carries a [small sample] L14 with 6.8 BB/9 red flag and a thin 61 IP AGG line. Market has BOS -124 which is fair; Yesavage's shiny 3.78 ERA masks a 4.31 xFIP. My fair line on BOS is roughly -130, only ~3 pts edge — under the gate.
picks.data_gap: Lineups unconfirmed; Yesavage L14 walk spike could be injury-related.

picks.reason: MIL is heavily favored and the lineup vs LHP is elite (136 wRC+, Vaughn/Chourio mashing) while CLE vs LHP is a brutal 48 wRC+. But MIL -147 already prices this; Drohan has only 18 IP AGG [small sample] which caps confidence. Messick is legitimately good (3.39 xFIP, 18.4 K-BB%) so I can't justify laying the price or backing the punchless CLE bats.
picks.data_gap: Roof status affects total only; Drohan small sample limits read.

picks.reason: Joe Ryan is a clear true-talent edge (3.27 SIERA, 22.9 K-BB%, 248 IP, Stf+108) over Leiter, whose AGG is mediocre (4.34 SIERA, 12.9 K-BB%) and whose L14 is a disaster. Market has this MIN +106 (no-vig ~46%) but Ryan's reliability against a fading Leiter pushes my MIN estimate to ~51.5%. MIN bats are average vs RHP and Ryan suppresses TEX's contact-heavy lineup in a pitcher park.
picks.data_gap: TEX bullpen T3 thin (2/5) but Latz/Junis fresh; lineups unconfirmed.

picks.reason: Woo is excellent (3.38 SIERA, 21.3 K-BB%, Stf+110) and underpriced by his 4.28 ERA, but SEA -149 already reflects this. Baz's L14 [small sample] shows velocity/K collapse (3.4 K/9) which is a red flag, yet BAL's strong vs-RHP lineup (124 wRC+) keeps this from being a layup. My SEA fair sits near -150, no edge. Two pitcher-friendly arms in a suppressive park lean the total under.
picks.data_gap: Roof status; Baz L14 K-rate drop needs velocity confirmation.

picks.reason: This is my favorite profile: Manaea's AGG is excellent (3.21 SIERA, 23.2 K-BB%, Stf+109) and his ugly 4.78 ERA is pure perception, while Nola's 5.86 ERA matches a deteriorating profile (L14 5.59 SIERA, 6.0 BB/9). Nola's AGG is decent but his recent form and CBP HR park (113) hurt him versus Soto/Young. Market is a near coin flip (+104) but I make NYM the slight favorite at ~53%.
picks.data_gap: Both starters carry L14 small-sample tags; lineups unconfirmed.

picks.reason: Burke's AGG is weak (4.67 SIERA, 10.4 K-BB%, Stf+93) and his L14 is unusable [small sample], facing the league's hottest offense in a HR park. NYY -157 is steep but probably fair given the talent gap; Weathers has a solid AGG (3.66 SIERA) but a poor L14. My NYY number lands near -160, no edge. Wind blowing and two contact-vulnerable arms lean over.
picks.data_gap: Lineups unconfirmed; Burke 4 IP L14 sample unreadable.
Weather/PPD is the decisive factor; will not bet under PPD risk.
